concerns.text: Assumptions section states 'For crossing number 13, the exact count is 49 prime knots, as established in OEIS A002863' but spec.md:Scope Boundaries and plan.md:Summary state '9988 prime knots at crossing number 13'. This 200x discrepancy fundamentally affects sample size expectations and power calculations. OEIS A002863 shows cumulative counts; verify whether 9988 refers to cumulative ≤13 or knots exactly at 13.

Composite complexity score (FR-006) is explicitly acknowledged as having 'no established mathematical basis' yet SC-003 requires validating its correlation with hyperbolic volume. This creates a construct validity gap: validating an exploratory construct without theoretical justification risks spurious correlations being treated as meaningful findings. Consider reframing as purely exploratory analysis without formal validation requirements.

Regression models predict hyperbolic volume from crossing number and braid index, but alternating/non-alternating classification is only used for stratified ANOVA (FR-008). If the relationship differs systematically between classes (the research question), this should be modeled as an interaction term in regression rather than separate stratified analyses alone.

Dataset Strategy table lists 'Knot Atlas Prime Knots' with source 'Knot Atlas (https://katlas.org)' but marked 'NO verified source found'. Constitution Principle II requires verified citations. Either verify this source or remove URL to avoid citation accuracy violations.
